_patch_meta.description: vcb-010 re-evaluation — MIXED terms (10 of 12). Strict-grouping discipline applied: prior 14 active groups across these 10 terms restructured into 41 characteristic-distinct groups. Routing rationale: legacy VERSECONTEXT bundles inserts and updates atomically under one input_versions check + md_version bump per §6.3 Step 3. NO-CHANGE terms G4123 (893) and H5951 (1096) emit a separate empty-ops VCREVISE patch.

record.context_description: Term names violent assault on inner being — the snatching-away of the implanted word from the heart, or the wolf-attack on souls under shepherd care
Term names forceful inner pursuit — the kingdom-of-heaven seized by force, the inner-being violence that lays hold of God's reign
Term names redemptive rescue by human agent — the snatching-out of others from fire-judgement context as an act of mercy with discriminating fear

set.context_description: Term names divine inviolability of those held in the Father's and Son's hand — the inability of any agent to seize the Christ-given inner-being possession
set.notes: Description revised in vcb-010 re-evaluation: prior 5493-001 bundled threat/security/rescue/pursuit. Restructured: 5493-001 retains divine-inviolability verses (Joh 10:28 related, Joh 10:29 anchor); other prior members redistributed to new 5493-003 (assault on inner being — Mat 13:19 anchor, Joh 10:12 related), 5493-004 (forceful inner pursuit — Mat 11:12 anchor sole), 5493-005 (redemptive rescue by human agent — Jude 23 anchor sole). 5493-002 retained, with Act 8:39 added (Spirit catching Philip away).

record.notes: Crowds intend to seize Jesus to make him king — physical-political seizure; harpazō does not carry inner-being content here. The crowd's flawed expectation and Jesus' withdrawal are inner-being matters carried by other lexical material in the verse, not by the target term itself.
Spirit of the Lord caught Philip away — divine snatching-away by the Spirit; same term-role as 1Th 4:17 / 2Cor 12:2 (divine catching-away of a person)
Soldiers tearing Paul out of the crowd at the Temple — physical military extraction; no inner-being engagement carried by the term

set.notes: Reassigned in vcb-010 from 5493-001 (related) to new 5493-004 (forceful inner pursuit — kingdom seized by force); promoted to anchor as sole verse of new group
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 5493-001 (anchor) to new 5493-003 (assault on inner being — evil one snatches the implanted word); retained as anchor of new group
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 5493-001 (related) to new 5493-003 (assault on inner being — wolf snatches the sheep)
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 5493-001 (related) to new 5493-005 (redemptive rescue by human agent); promoted to anchor as sole verse of new group

record.context_description: Term names the external plundering accepted with inner joy — the loss occasioned by faith that has secured 'a better possession'

set.context_description: Term names rapacity as inner moral condition — the inner fullness of greedy desire that the Pharisees' outward propriety conceals
set.notes: Description revised in vcb-010 re-evaluation: prior 892-001 bundled inner-rapacity (Mat/Lk) and external-plundering-accepted-with-joy (Heb 10:34) as opposites. Restructured: 892-001 retains inner-rapacity verses (Mat 23:25 anchor, Luk 11:39 related); 892-002 NEW with Heb 10:34 (external plundering accepted with inner joy) as sole verse / anchor.
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 892-001 (related) to new 892-002 (external plundering accepted with inner joy); promoted to anchor as sole verse of new group

record.context_description: Term names the casting-off of a physical obstacle as expression of inner urgency toward Christ — the eager approach disclosed through the discarded impediment

set.context_description: Term names the abandonment of an inner disposition — confidence as something the inner person can throw away, warned against in pursuit of the promised reward
set.notes: Description revised in vcb-010 re-evaluation: prior 6115-001 bundled physical-obstacle expression and inner-disposition abandonment. Restructured into 6115-001 (Heb 10:35 anchor — inner disposition abandonment) and 6115-002 (Mar 10:50 anchor — physical-obstacle expression of inner urgency).
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 6115-001 (related) to new 6115-002 (physical-obstacle expression of inner urgency); promoted to anchor as sole verse of new group

record.notes: Loss of life / ship in shipwreck context; apobolē names physical loss with no inner-being engagement here. STEP/Mounce explicitly distinguishes the two senses (loss in Acts 27:22; rejection in Rom 11:15)

record.context_description: Term names refusal of moral truth — inner non-conformity that prefers unrighteousness
Term names corporate covenantal disobedience in salvation-history — the persistent inner refusal of a people that occasioned judgement or exclusion
Term names covenantal disobedience as relational state in Paul's mercy-logic — the prior or current state that mercy is shown into

set.context_description: Term names refusal of the Son / word / gospel — inner-being refusal directed at the divine self-revelation in Christ
set.notes: Description revised in vcb-010 re-evaluation: prior 1199-001 'Wilful inner refusal — disobedience as an act of the will toward God, his Son, or his word' was correct in spirit but bundled multiple objects of refusal. Restructured: 1199-001 retains refusal-of-revelation verses (Joh 3:36 anchor, 1Pe 2:8, 1Pe 3:1, 1Pe 4:17); 1199-003 carries refusal of moral truth (Rom 2:8); 1199-004 carries corporate covenantal disobedience (Rom 10:21 anchor, Heb 3:18, Heb 11:31, 1Pe 3:20); 1199-005 carries Pauline mercy-logic disobedience (Rom 11:30 anchor, Rom 11:31).

set.context_description: Term names active unbelief expressing itself in hostility against gospel-bearers — the persistent inner refusal that issues in inter-personal aggression
set.notes: Description revised in vcb-010 re-evaluation: prior 1199-002 bundled active hostility (Acts 14:2, Acts 19:9, Rom 15:31) with Pauline mercy-logic verses (Rom 11:30, 11:31). The mercy-logic verses moved to new 1199-005; revised 1199-002 holds only active-hostility verses. Anchor reassigned from Rom 11:30 (now in 1199-005) to Acts 19:9.
Promoted to anchor of revised 1199-002 (active unbelief expressing as hostility) — replaces Rom 11:30 which moved to new 1199-005
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1199-001 (related) to new 1199-003 (refusal of moral truth); promoted to anchor as sole verse of new group
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1199-001 (related) to new 1199-004 (corporate covenantal disobedience); promoted to anchor of new group
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1199-001 (related) to new 1199-004 (corporate covenantal disobedience — wilderness generation)
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1199-001 (related) to new 1199-004 (corporate covenantal disobedience — Jericho)
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1199-001 (related) to new 1199-004 (corporate covenantal disobedience — Noah-era)
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1199-002 (anchor) to new 1199-005 (covenantal disobedience as state mercy is shown into); retained as anchor of new group
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1199-002 (related) to new 1199-005 (covenantal disobedience as state mercy is shown into)

record.context_description: Term names disciples' post-resurrection unbelief under apostolic-formation rebuke — the inner state of the appointed witnesses being corrected
Term names hypothetical covenantal infidelity as rhetorical foil — Israelite faithlessness invoked argumentatively against God's unfailing faithfulness
Term names the inner state in negated role — the unbelief that did not arise to govern Abraham's response, against which faith grew strong
Term names continuing-but-not-permanent corporate unbelief — the inner state as a condition that may be discontinued, holding the door open to restoration
Term names autobiographical pre-conversion unbelief — the prior inner state into which mercy was given, ground for the apostolic confession
Term names the heart's susceptibility to an unbelieving condition leading to apostasy — directive warning to believers about the inner trajectory away from the living God

set.context_description: Term names corporate covenantal unbelief — the inner state of a people that issues in covenantal-historical limitation or exclusion
set.notes: Description revised in vcb-010 re-evaluation: prior 1198-001 bundled multiple distinct roles. Revised 1198-001 holds only corporate-with-consequence verses (Mat 13:58, Mar 6:6, Rom 11:20, Heb 3:19); other prior members redistributed to 1198-002 (Mar 9:24 self-acknowledged) and new 1198-003, -004, -006, -007, -008. Anchor reassigned from Heb 3:12 (now in 1198-008) to Heb 3:19.

set.context_description: Term names individual self-acknowledged unbelief co-existing with faith — the inner state confessed in personal struggle and brought to Christ for help
set.notes: Description revised in vcb-010 re-evaluation: prior 1198-002 'persistent inner state with decisive consequences' was role-imprecise. Revised 1198-002 holds Mar 9:24 only; other prior members redistributed to revised 1198-001 (Rom 11:20, Heb 3:19) and new 1198-005 (Rom 4:20), 1198-006 (Rom 11:23). Anchor reassigned from Rom 4:20 (now in 1198-005) to Mar 9:24.
Promoted to anchor of revised 1198-002 (individual self-acknowledged unbelief co-existing with faith)
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1198-001 (related) to new 1198-003 (apostolic-formation rebuke); promoted to anchor as sole member
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1198-001 (related) to new 1198-004 (rhetorical foil to God's faithfulness); promoted to anchor as sole member
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1198-002 (anchor) to new 1198-005 (negated role / Abraham); retained as anchor of new group
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1198-002 (related) to revised 1198-001 (corporate covenantal unbelief — Israel broken off)
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1198-002 (related) to new 1198-006 (continuing-but-not-permanent unbelief / grafting condition); promoted to anchor as sole member
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1198-001 (related) to new 1198-007 (autobiographical pre-conversion unbelief / mercy ground); promoted to anchor as sole member
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1198-001 (anchor) to new 1198-008 (heart-susceptibility / directive-warning); retained as anchor of new group

Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1198-002 (related) to revised 1198-001 (corporate covenantal unbelief — wilderness exclusion); promoted to anchor of revised 1198-001 — replaces Heb 3:12 which moved to 1198-008

record.context_description: Term intensifies a cohortative directed at shared action — particle that discloses the inner urgency in 'let us' speech, framing the volitional response to prior revelation or pastoral concern
Term intensifies a divine imperative under prophetic-prayer setting — particle that sharpens the moment-of-decision quality of a Spirit-given command
Term intensifies an indicative declaration about inner-being reception — particle that sharpens the contrast between fruit-bearing hearing-with-understanding and the prior failed reception

set.context_description: Term intensifies an inferential link from identity-fact to embodied-volitional response — particle that grounds the imperative in the indicative ('you were bought with a price; so glorify God in your body')
set.notes: Description revised in vcb-010 re-evaluation: prior 6602-001 'dē intensifying an inner-being directive or volitional divine command' was role-imprecise. Revised 6602-001 retains 1Cor 6:20 (inferential-grounding) as anchor; other functional roles distinguished into 6602-002 (cohortative-urgency, Luk 2:15 + Act 15:36), 6602-003 (divine-imperative-intensifying, Act 13:2), 6602-004 (indicative-emphasis, Mat 13:23).

record.notes: dē intensifies the indicative contrast between good-soil hearer (with-understanding, fruit-bearing) and prior failed soils; particle sharpens the inner-being-reception emphasis
dē with cohortative ('let us go over') discloses shepherds' inner urgency to act on the angelic revelation
dē with cohortative ('let us return and visit') discloses Paul's pastoral urgency for the churches

set.notes: Reassigned in vcb-010 from 6602-001 (related) to new 6602-003 (divine-imperative-intensifying); promoted to anchor as sole verse of new group

record.context_description: Term names pastoral-relational urgency expressed in travel imperatives — the apostolic-protégé eagerness for personal presence under separation or extremity (Pauline epistolary 'do your best to come' idiom)

set.context_description: Term names inner-disposition of eager striving toward a spiritual, formative, or eschatological end — diligence pressing toward inner-being formation, kingdom obedience, ecclesial unity, or eschatological readiness
set.notes: Description revised in vcb-010 re-evaluation: prior 4465-001 was generic. Two characteristic-distinct uses identified — substantive spiritual/formative eagerness retained in 4465-001 (8 verses); pastoral-travel-urgency idiom split into new 4465-002 (3 verses: 2Ti 4:9, 4:21, Tit 3:12). 2Ti 2:15 retained in 4465-001 because object is presenting-to-God-as-approved (formative), not travel.
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 4465-001 (related) to new 4465-002 (pastoral-travel-urgency); promoted to anchor of new group
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 4465-001 (related) to new 4465-002 (pastoral-travel-urgency idiom — 'before winter')
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 4465-001 (related) to new 4465-002 (pastoral-travel-urgency idiom — 'come to me at Nicopolis')

record.context_description: Term names inner earnestness as a tested and intensifying character quality — diligence proven across many matters and now heightened by confidence in the addressees

set.context_description: Term names inner earnestness as voluntary self-motivation — the disposition that goes beyond what was asked, expressed in going 'of one's own accord'
set.notes: Description revised in vcb-010 re-evaluation: prior description was tuned to 2Cor 8:22 (tested-character) but materially misrepresented 2Cor 8:17 (anchor — voluntariness, 'of his own accord'). Two single-verse groups: 4467-001 (voluntariness — 2Cor 8:17) and 4467-002 (tested-ness — 2Cor 8:22).
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 4467-001 (related) to new 4467-002 (tested-and-intensifying character quality); promoted to anchor as sole verse of new group

record.context_description: Term names inner zeal as the right Christian disposition for body-life — fervency of spirit appropriate to gift-exercise and contrasted with sloth
Term names earnestness as the inner-being fruit produced by godly grief — the disposition that arises in true repentance
Term names earnestness revealed under apostolic disciplinary writing — the relational disposition disclosed in the sight of God through corrective letter
Term names earnestness as one virtue among others in which a church excels — the inner disposition listed alongside faith, speech, knowledge, and love
Term names earnestness as the evidence of genuine love — the inner disposition that proves love by comparison with others
Term names earnest care as God-given inner disposition — the heart-implanted same-care of one minister mirroring another
Term names earnestness oriented toward eschatological assurance — the inner disposition pursuing full assurance of hope until the end
Term names inner effort directed at moral-formative supplementation — making every effort to supplement faith with virtue and virtue with knowledge

set.context_description: Term names physical haste expressing negative-moral inner urgency — the haste of compliance with a murderous demand
set.notes: Description revised in vcb-010 re-evaluation: prior 1268-001 was generic. Restructured: 1268-001 holds Mar 6:25 alone (negative-moral haste); other prior members redistributed to 1268-002 (Luk 1:39 — positive-obedient haste), new 1268-004 (Rom 12:8, 12:11 — body-life zeal), new 1268-005 (2Cor 7:11 — fruit-of-godly-grief).

set.context_description: Term names physical haste expressing positive-obedient inner eagerness — the haste of joyful response after divine revelation
set.notes: Description revised in vcb-010 re-evaluation: prior 1268-002 ('earnestness produced by godly grief') redistributed across new groups. Revised 1268-002 holds Luk 1:39 alone (positive-obedient haste). Prior members redistributed to new 1268-006 through 1268-011. Anchor reassigned from 2Cor 8:8 (now in 1268-008) to Luk 1:39.

set.context_description: Term names the author's inner-eagerness as urgency-of-writing-desire — the disposition disclosed in autobiographical address
set.notes: Description revised in vcb-010 re-evaluation: same single member (Jude 3); description re-purposed for clarity to name the autobiographical-authorial inner urgency disclosed in the address.
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1268-001 (related) to revised 1268-002 (positive-obedient haste); promoted to anchor as sole verse
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1268-001 (related) to new 1268-004 (body-life zeal — gift exercise)
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1268-001 (related) to new 1268-004 (body-life zeal — corporate fervency); promoted to anchor of new group
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1268-001 (related) to new 1268-005 (fruit of godly grief); promoted to anchor as sole verse
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1268-001 (related) to new 1268-006 (revealed under disciplinary writing); promoted to anchor as sole verse
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1268-001 (related) to new 1268-007 (catalogue-virtue); promoted to anchor as sole verse
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1268-002 (anchor) to new 1268-008 (evidence of genuine love); retained as anchor of new group
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1268-002 (related) to new 1268-009 (God-given earnest care); promoted to anchor as sole verse
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1268-002 (related) to new 1268-010 (eschatological assurance); promoted to anchor as sole verse
Reassigned in vcb-010 from 1268-002 (related) to new 1268-011 (moral-formative supplementation); promoted to anchor as sole verse
